paper_title: Materials and Methods of Arctic Sediment Analysis

methods.description: Collection and preparation of sediment core samples from the Canada Basin for various analyses.

steps.description: Gravity core ARC4-BN05 was collected from the Canada Basin in the vicinity of the Mendeleev Ridge.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment core samples from Canada Basin

steps.description: A total of 119 samples were taken at 2 cm intervals over the 238 cm BN05 length, and kept frozen until analyzed.
steps.searchable_summary: segment and store sediment core samples

methods.description: Age constraint within the radiocarbon range using accelerator mass spectrometry 14C dating on planktic foraminifers.

steps.description: Accelerator mass spectrometry 14C dating was performed on 1000-1200 tests of planktic foraminifers.

methods.description: Analysis of sediment grain size after chemical treatment to remove organic matter, carbonates, and silica.

steps.description: Sediment samples were treated with H2O2, HCl, and Na2CO3 to remove organic matter, biogenic carbonates, and biogenic silica.
steps.searchable_summary: chemically treat sediment samples to remove impurities

steps.description: Grain-size measurements were performed on a Malvern Mastersize laser particle sizer.
steps.searchable_summary: measure grain size using laser particle sizer

methods.description: Determination of major element concentrations using ICP-OES and XRF core scanning.

steps.description: Concentrations of major elements were determined by ICP-OES.
Relative elemental abundances were obtained using the Itrax XRF core scanner.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain elemental abundances using XRF core scanner

methods.description: Measurement of magnetic susceptibility and demagnetization of natural remanent magnetization for paleomagnetic analysis.

steps.description: Magnetic susceptibility was measured using the KLY-4s Kappabridge instrument.
Stepwise alternating-field demagnetization of natural remanent magnetization was conducted.

methods.description: Analysis of bulk sediment mineralogy using X-ray diffraction.

steps.description: Samples were dried, pulverized, sieved, and loaded into aluminum holders for XRD analysis.
Samples were X-rayed and peak areas were estimated using Jade 6.0 software.
steps.searchable_summary: perform XRD and estimate peak areas

methods.description: Determination of clay mineral composition using X-ray diffraction and glycolation.

steps.description: Samples were treated with H2O2 and HCl, and clay fractions were obtained by the Atterberg settling tubes method.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare clay samples for XRD analysis

steps.description: Clay minerals were identified by XRD using a D/max-2500 diffractometer.

methods.description: PCA performed to identify potential contributions from various sediment sources and interpret downcore proxy distributions.

steps.description: PCA was performed in MATLAB including all analyzed mineralogical proxies and main grain-size groups.
